notes: NI Session Bassist series — pattern-based Kontakt bass instruments. Three variants share the same CC layout but differ in character: Icon Bass (modern electric, punchy/mid-focused), Prime Bass (versatile electric, studio-ready), Upright Bass (acoustic double bass, warm/woody). All variants are pattern-driven: MIDI notes in the lower range trigger grooves and fills; notes in the upper range trigger live melodic playing mode. CC 11 (Expression) is the primary level/dynamics control. CC 64 switches between chord hold and immediate mute on pattern release. The Quick Controls adjust tone, pickup character, and performance behaviour — exact knob labels vary by variant but CC numbers are consistent. Right-click any QC knob in Kontakt → Learn MIDI CC to verify for your loaded patch.

Expression.description: Expression — primary dynamics/level control. Automate for verse/chorus level differences and natural bass swell into a section.

Modulation.description: Performance variation or groove intensity — instrument-dependent. On some patches selects between playing styles (e.g. pick vs fingerstyle blend).

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds pattern loop running / chord sustain; release to mute/choke

Quick Control 1.description: Pickup Position — bridge pickup (bright, cutting, attack-forward) vs neck pickup (warm, round, smooth). On Upright Bass: bow pressure / arco–pizzicato blend.

Quick Control 2.description: Tone / EQ Character — overall tonal brightness. Low = dark and vintage. High = modern hi-fi snap. On Upright: body resonance.

Quick Control 3.description: Amp / DI Blend — direct signal vs amp cabinet character. High = more amp warmth and grit; low = clean DI punch.

Quick Control 4.description: Reverb / Room Amount — built-in reverb mix. Keep low for tight modern bass; raise for a more vintage roomy feel.

Quick Control 5.description: Attack Speed — transient shaper; how hard the pick or pluck hits. High = snappy, percussive. Low = smooth, soft onset.

Quick Control 6.description: Release / Sustain Time — note decay tail length after key-up. Short = tight and staccato; long = legato sustain.

Quick Control 7.description: Fret / String Noise — mechanical noise level: fret slides, string buzz, finger release. Add for rawer, more live feel.

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity, timing, and tuning variation between notes for a more played-in feel

Portamento.description: Portamento/glide time for legato slides between notes
